name: Jumbo CLI
description: Local-first context orchestration CLI for coding agents. Event-sourced architecture with goal lifecycle management, multi-agent support, and structured project memory.
tagline: Project context orchestration for coding agents
approachDetail: Event sourcing with CQRS — append-only JSONL event store as source of truth, SQLite read projections for querying. Entities: components, dependencies, decisions, goals, relations
searchDetail: SQL LIKE pattern matching with wildcard support, filtering by name/type/status, free-text on description fields
storageDetail: Dual-store: filesystem JSONL event files per aggregate UUID (source of truth) + SQLite projection (rebuildable). All data in local .jumbo/ directory
toolSupportDetail: Auto-configures hooks and instruction files per agent (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, copilot-instructions.md, etc.)
scopesDetail: Session scope for active work context with goals; project scope via .jumbo/ directory. No global/cross-project scope
retentionDetail: All events persist indefinitely. No TTL or archival policies
multiUserDetail: .jumbo/ is gitignored by default, local-only. Cloud/team version planned
crossMachineDetail: Local-only. No sync or remote backend
tokenBudgetingDetail: No token counting or budget management
contextInjectionDetail: Generates AGENTS.md instruction files that agents read at session start; auto-configures hooks per agent
auditTrailDetail: Event-sourced architecture: append-only JSONL event store with full history of every change
pros: Scaffolds native instruction files per agent (CLAUDE.md, copilot-instructions.md, GEMINI.md) — no MCP wiring needed
Event sourcing gives full audit trail
Structured goal lifecycle (define → refine → implement → review → codify)
Zero network calls, fully local and private
Rich entity model (components, decisions, dependencies, relations)
cons: No cross-machine sync or team support
No duplicate detection or retention policies
Relies on instruction files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md) which agents can ignore — no structured API contract
Context grows with project size — no token budgeting or selective retrieval
